2026-04-23T08:09:36.9539169Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9539743Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9540175Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9540562Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9540881Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9541118Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9541279Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9541458Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9541628Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9541857Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T08:09:36.9542344Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T08:09:36.9542617Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-23T08:13:41.6700820Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T08:10:37.548041638Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58571,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Link and surface new @`@clientOption` reference page in README and docs index\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (#57924) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57924 added `resource-name-constraint @`@clientOption`` support to the Azure management-plane C# emitter. As part of this work, a new reference page was added at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` documenting two supported `@`@clientOption`` keys:\\n\\n- `resource-rbac-roles` — defines RBAC role definitions for provisioning\\n- `resource-name-constraint` *(newly added)* — overrides resource name min/max length and character pattern constraints\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The top-level `README.md` at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption``, `resource-name-constraint`, or `resource-rbac-roles`. There is no link to `client-options.md`, making the feature undiscoverable for spec authors reading the README.\\n- The `docs/index.mdx` only links to `emitter.md` (\\u0026#34;See documentation\\u0026#34;) and does not reference `client-options.md`. Spec authors navigating the published docs will not find the supported `@`@clientOption`` keys.\\n- No \\u0026#34;Supported decorators\\u0026#34; or \\u0026#34;Advanced customization\\u0026#34; section exists in the README to guide spec authors who need to customize resource name constraints or RBAC roles.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** this file. After the existing emitter options section (the last option is `skip-api-version-override`), add a new section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\nThe emitter recognizes additional metadata for resource code generation via the TCGC [`@`@clientOption``]((azure.github.io/redacted) decorator scoped to `\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;`.\\n\\nFor the full reference with examples and generated output, see [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md).\\n\\n### `resource-rbac-roles`\\n\\nDefines RBAC role definitions for a resource. Used by the provisioning generator to emit `BuiltInRole` structs and `CreateRoleAssignment` methods.\\n\\n### `resource-name-constraint`\\n\\nOverrides resource name constraints (min length, max length, and valid character pattern). Useful when adding `@pattern`, `@minLength`, or `@maxLength` decorators directly to the spec would cause breaking changes in the generated swagger.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx`\\n\\n**Edit** this file. Change the existing `## Emitter usage` section\\u0026#39;s documentation link from the current:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\n[See reference](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this change. The content in `client-options.md` already contains TypeSpec and C# code examples. The README additions should be plain markdown prose with inline links — no `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling is needed.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `README.md` for this package is a tooling/generator README, not a client SDK README, so it does not need the standard Getting Started / Key Concepts / Examples / Troubleshooting / Next Steps / Contributing structure. However, verify that the new section:\\n\\n1. Is placed at the end of the emitter options, before any `## Contributing` or closing section.\\n2. Uses correct relative link paths (e.g., `./docs/client-options.md`).\\n3. Does not duplicate content already present in `client-options.md` — the README should describe the feature briefly and link to the reference file.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; npm install` — ensure dependencies are installed\\n2. `npm run lint` — verify no lint errors in emitter or docs\\n3. `npm run prettier` — verify formatting\\n\\nThere are no C# build or test steps required for this documentation-only change. The `Export-API.ps1` and `Update-Snippets.ps1` scripts are not applicable here (this is an emitter/generator package, not a C# client SDK).\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; As part of this work, a new reference page was added at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` documenting two supported `@`@clientOption`` keys:\\n\\n- `resource-rbac-roles` — defines RBAC role definitions for provisioning\\n- `resource-name-constraint` *(newly added)* — overrides resource name min/max length and character pattern constraints\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The top-level `README.md` at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption``, `resource-name-constraint`, or `resource-rbac-roles`. There is no link to `client-options.md`, making the feature undiscoverable for spec authors reading the README.\\n- The `docs/index.mdx` only links to `emitter.md` (\\u0026#34;See documentation\\u0026#34;) and does not reference `client-options.md`. Spec authors navigating the published docs will not find the supported `@`@clientOption`` keys.\\n- No \\u0026#34;Supported decorators\\u0026#34; or \\u0026#34;Advanced customization\\u0026#34; section exists in the README to guide spec authors who need to customize resource name constraints or RBAC roles.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** this file. After the existing emitter options section (the last option is `skip-api-version-override`), add a new section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\nThe emitter recognizes additional metadata for resource code generation via the TCGC [`@`@clientOption``]((azure.github.io/redacted) decorator scoped to `\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;`.\\n\\nFor the full reference with examples and generated output, see [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md).\\n\\n### `resource-rbac-roles`\\n\\nDefines RBAC role definitions for a resource. Used by the provisioning generator to emit `BuiltInRole` structs and `CreateRoleAssignment` methods.\\n\\n### `resource-name-constraint`\\n\\nOverrides resource name constraints (min length, max length, and valid character pattern). Useful when adding `@pattern`, `@minLength`, or `@maxLength` decorators directly to the spec would cause breaking changes in the generated swagger.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx`\\n\\n**Edit** this file. Change the existing `## Emitter usage` section\\u0026#39;s documentation link from the current:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\n[See reference](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this change. The content in `client-options.md` already contains TypeSpec and C# code examples. The README additions should be plain markdown prose with inline links — no `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling is needed.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `README.md` for this package is a tooling/generator README, not a client SDK README, so it does not need the standard Getting Started / Key Concepts / Examples / Troubleshooting / Next Steps / Contributing structure. However, verify that the new section:\\n\\n1. Is placed at the end of the emitter options, before any `## Contributing` or closing section.\\n2. Uses correct relative link paths (e.g., `./docs/client-options.md`).\\n3. Does not duplicate content already present in `client-options.md` — the README should describe the feature briefly and link to the reference file.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; npm install` — ensure dependencies are installed\\n2. `npm run lint` — verify no lint errors in emitter or docs\\n3. `npm run prettier` — verify formatting\\n\\nThere are no C# build or test steps required for this documentation-only change. The `Export-API.ps1` and `Update-Snippets.ps1` scripts are not applicable here (this is an emitter/generator package, not a C# client SDK).\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt;
